title: philadelphus microphyllus subsp. typicus
intro: Philadelphus microphyllus subsp. typicus, also known as the Dwarf Mock Orange, is a shrub species belonging to the Hydrangeaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The Dwarf Mock Orange offers various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
